elements: camera body represented as a rectangular prism
lens opening at the front of the camera body
Z-axis running through the center of the lens parallel to camera translation
horizontal arrow cluster pointing forwards along the Z-axis to denote forward translation
light rays originating from a distant scene converging towards the lens
image plane inside the camera behind the lens
origin point (0, 0, 0) at the center of the camera body
alignment grid in the background to illustrate parallel movement

interactions.label: Translate Z
interactions.teaches: Adjusts the forward translation of the camera along the Z-axis, demonstrating how the perspective and distance from the scene change as the camera moves closer or farther from the subject
Hovering over the camera provides insight into how its internal components relate to its external movement

demo_steps.title: Initial Position
demo_steps.narration: The camera is at its starting position, perfectly aligned with the background grid. The light rays converge towards the lens, forming an image on the interior image plane.

demo_steps.title: Forward Movement
demo_steps.narration: As the camera begins to move forward along the Z-axis, notice how the light rays shift in relation to the lens. This changes the scene's perspective on the image plane accordingly.

demo_steps.title: Closer to the Subject
demo_steps.narration: Further translation reduces the distance to the scene, magnifying the perceived size and detail on the image plane, akin to zooming in.

plan.camera_suggestion: Align camera view to face directly towards the lens from the Z-axis, ensuring horizontal arrows and alignment grid maintain the parallelism and spacing of the original figure.

inline_constraints.explainers: hover tooltips for insight about components and movement
inline_constraints.framing: match the source crop and whitespace; do not force-fill the iframe if the original has margins
inline_constraints.camera: align the initial viewpoint to match the original figure's framing and perspective

plan.notes: Ensure light ray density and camera position are accurate precisions of the original scene.

elements: 3D camera model at origin along the Z-axis
multiple concentric circles on the image plane representing different depths
focus of expansion point (x_infinity, y_infinity) on the image plane
motion vectors extending radially from the focus of expansion
grid representing a wall directly ahead in the Z-axis, with motion vectors
arrows representing the direction and magnitude of motion across the grid
Z-axis with arrow indicating forward motion

interactions.label: Depth Z
interactions.teaches: Changing the depth of objects demonstrates how motion magnitude decreases with distance due to 1/Z scaling.
Illustrates that as the camera moves forward, points appear to move radially outward from the focus of expansion.

demo_steps.title: Forward motion setup
demo_steps.narration: Imagine a camera moving forward along the Z-axis toward a wall. The closer points on the wall appear to move faster outward from the center, while distant points barely move.

demo_steps.title: Decreasing depth, faster motion
demo_steps.narration: As objects get closer to the camera, their apparent motion on the image plane increases. This demonstrates the inverse relationship between depth and motion magnitude: 1/Z.

demo_steps.title: Focus of expansion observed
demo_steps.narration: Points on the wall directly in line with the camera's trajectory appear stationary as the camera approaches them. This is the focus of expansion — the optical center of the projected motion field.

plan.camera_suggestion: Initial viewpoint from above the X and Y axes looking down at the Z-axis, showing the full setup including the camera and wall with apparent motion vectors.

inline_constraints.framing: match the source crop and whitespace; do not force-fill the iframe if the original has margins

plan.notes: Use a perspective camera to emphasize the parallax effect. Ensure motion vectors update accurately with changes in depth. Arrows should scale proportionally with depth adjustment, demonstrating changes in apparent velocity effectively.
